content:
I wanna keep a final point on topic now. A lot of the Reddit and twitter users are complaining about the difficulty going back up...

I suppose it's not hard to see where they're coming from. People saying they were stuck before chapter 8. Meanwhile again, one of my favourite parts from playing the game was overcoming that.

It definitely shouldn't be as hard as it was. But not as easy as it is. Even if the balance takes a few patches to find. Dats fine.

content:
It's all about the developers intended experience. Ig.

Even the removed madness debuffs were first added in with some intension in mind. Like hp cacoons. As much as the community hated them. They allowed the bosses to actually showcase all their mechanics. And players were forced to play/think around them. And probably even unconsciously created experiences that are looked back on fondly. Rather then gacha game boss hp stick dies in a few hits.

This currently is the equitable to a irl remake of old Disney movies, where half of the intention feels lost in translation.

Arc 2 obviously doesn't face this issue purely because... Boss hp is massive. And everyone is expected to be endgame kitted out for it already.

content:
I really appreciate your transparency, and I completely understand how difficult it is for the devs to balance gameplay experience and player satisfaction/retention. Especially during a critical window like the anniversary.

As a newer player, I happened to be on Arc 1 Chapter 9 right before the nerf, and it was a bit shocking how easy it was to defeat the final bosses. The story sadly lost some of its impact for me because it wasn't challenging, so I agree that the nerf was too drastic. But I'm still very happy to support the game, and I have faith that you and the dev team will find a good balance eventually. Wishing you well!

content:
Alright. I'll give my own arguments a counter argument. Pertaining specifically to the situation of my friends who have problems with the balancing now.

A lot of them have been just doing dailies, trapbase and events for a while. Waiting for the patch and still expecting the same/similar difficulty.

Their units are a lot more built up then new players have at those points of the game...

So the normal mode balancing might be fine... As much as I hate to admit it...

thread_title: 前瞻：V2.4.0.X 挑战性调整 Preview: V2.4.0.X Challenge Adjustments

content:
Can't comment on the business side because i don't know what you know - but must say that i am playing this game solely because it is challenging while giving a lot of tools and agency to the player. While you pinned a statement about comitting to offering challenge in new/top tier content - i am worried about the many signs of slop (auto battle, brainrot onboarding through rewards, absurd nerf) i am seeing. I left Wuwa in 1.3-1.4 when it became slop instead of genshin but its dark souls, and i would be sad about having to leave Morimens soon because it became slop instead of slay the spire but live service. Give me ridiculous complexity and 40 minutes turns Astrea style, clearing bosses in 1 hour and 28 turns with high stakes back and forth is why im playing. Based on my attempt at playing story today i just want to stop playing, because i cant call it playing, i click on buttons then i win. Please stay elitist, and accept that the bottom 20% weaklings dont belong here, minimum. Nerfing to be a little more welcoming is understandable, attempting to capture a "wider audience" is a mistake.

New Pdive being calibrated around speed clearing/efficiency only is also disappointing on the other hand of the spectum- i want to explore slow, deliberate playstyles, and perform crazy combos, not just see red numbers go up through nukes

We have a lot of awakeners and creatice team combinations that are possible, but if the only way to improve performance is nukes, then why ?

前瞻：V2.4.0.X 挑战性调整 Preview: V2.4.0.X Challenge Adjustments

content:
I agree. I only started playing this game recently because people were talking about it as one of the hardest gacha. For me it really was a fun experience, and I managed to finish 4 story chapters and 3 hard chapters before the patch. Yesterday I cleared Chapter 5 and it was braindead easy, even compared to the early chapters that basically serve as a tutorial. It’s not like I was using meta units with enlightenments either; I was mostly playing with standard characters without any dupes.

I figured the real challenge would be in hard mode, but then I found out that it was heavily butchered as well. 

Ngl, I’m disappointed, and it really killed my motivation to keep playing despite the very good story. There are plenty of gacha slops where you just turn on auto-battle and go make yourself a coffee without using your brain at all, probably around 99% of the market, but truly difficult and interesting games? I can barely name any.
